name: ModeCheck
description: Global check for mode change warnings while still in process mode

name: InletPressures
description:
Mix Process mode inlet pressure option.
     Minimum: allow any feed inlet pressure. The outlet pressure is then set to the minimum inlet pressure.
     Balanced: automatically set all feed inlet pressures equal. Therefore, exactly one pressure specification
     on the feed line must be freed up, typically a valve's, a pipe's or an exchanger's DP specification.
For Fluid Flow and Dynamic modes all feed inlet pressures are automatically balanced.

name: RxnRateBasis
description:
Global fluid equilibrium reaction rate basis.
     Absolute: Reaction rates represent absolute molar rates of change. 
     Per Unit Feed: Reaction rates are normalized based on the feed flow 
         rate. Representing the molar change per unit feed, they become
         independent of the absolute flow rate (preferred).

Both options yield identical results, but require different formulations
in the reaction Rate model. “Per Unit Feed” is recommended due to
better numerical stability when stream flows approach zero rate.
Starting with v2023 fluid reactions are defaulted to the "Per Unit Feed"
option.  The "Absolute" option is retained for compatibility with
simulations built before v2023 and will be removed in a future release.

name: Elevations
description:
Global selection to enable the specification of equipment port elevations.
     Disabled: disable specifications for port elevations.  The elevation change (DE) can still be specified in the Pipe and PipeRig models.
     Manual: manually specify port elevations. DE in the Pipe and PipeRig models can be swapped for port elevation specifications.
     Automatic: automatically calculate port elevations using equipment geometry and configuration options for select models (Column, Drum, etc).

When automatic elevations are enabled, an elevation specification must be removed from each inlet stream of impacted models (for example, remove the DE specification from an inlet Pipe).
Other model elevations must still be manually specified. For an explanation of how port elevations are estimated, see each model's help file.

name: InletElevations
description:
Global selection to determine the equipment elevation from the connected inlet elevations.
     Ignore: use the maximum elevation in the inlet flow path as the equipment elevation. All other inlet elevations are then ignored.
     Equalize: all inlet elevations are forced to be equal. Therefore, an elevation related specification in all but one inlet flow paths
     must be unspecified. This variable is typically a DE variable in a HX, Pipe, PFR. 
 
To set InletElevation to 'Equalize' proceed as follows: 
- set InletElevation to 'Equalize'
- initially, the simulation will go unsquare and you will need to identify all Mixer, Header and CompSplit models.
- focus only on Mixer, Header, CompSplit models fed by inlet flow paths that have equipment models.
  with elevation difference DE variables, e.g. HX, Pipe, PFR.
- focus only on all but one of their inlet flow paths.
- in these flow paths, remove the specification on the DE variables of equipment models, e.g. HX, Pipe, PFR.

name: Volume
description: Mix option to turn on header volume. Use for Dynamic simulation when using pipe.\n     Holdup: use this option to account for volume holdup when using Pipe.\n     Instantaneous: use this option to ignore volume holdup when using PipeRig

name: Phases
description:
Mix option to override the fluid phase selection
     Fluid: The FluidType's phases are applied.
     Vapor: The phases are set to Vapor.
     Liquid: The phases are set to Liquid.
     VLE: The phases are set to VLE.
     VLWE: The phases are set to VLWE.
     VLLE: The phases are set to VLLE.

name: PhaseNoOverride
description: Mix PhaseNoOverride parameter defaulted to NoOverride which is used for the connection to the State.PhaseOverride when Phases = Fluid
